experiments.variant: Why Does This Fish Have White Blood? #Shorts
experiments.evalNotes: Resolved manually after evaluator escalation. The video had ZERO traffic in both the pre-test baseline (2026-06-15 to 2026-07-03: 0 views every day except 3 views on 2026-06-16) and the variant period (2026-07-04 to 2026-07-06: 0 views). With no traffic either before or after the title change, the test is inconclusive — there is no signal to measure the variant's effect. Decision: KEEP the variant title (do not revert). Rationale: (1) no evidence the variant hurt since there was no traffic to lose; (2) the variant framing 'Why Does This Fish Have White Blood?' is a stronger curiosity-gap hook than the baseline 'Antarctic Fish with White Blood', so it is the better bet when traffic returns; (3) reverting would be churn with no supporting data. The evaluator cron's 'empty rows' was this zero-traffic case (a 0-view video returns no row on a dimension=video query), not an API/auth failure. Evaluator cron updated to treat zero-traffic variant periods as inconclusive/keep instead of escalating.

experiments.baseline: Why the Penguin Survives the Coldest Winter on Earth #Shorts
experiments.variant: Why the Penguin Stands in the Dark at Minus 40 for Two Months #Shorts
experiments.launchedBy: agent (manual launch — Pulse cron 8c24d1c2 failed to auto-launch on 2026-07-22 due to lifetime-views eligibility gap; launched interactively per fully-automatic policy autoLaunchTitleTests=true)

baselineMetrics.note: High views (1253, well above 200 threshold) but below-median retention (18s vs ~27s channel median) — viewers click but bounce

experiments.rationale: Variant swaps generic 'coldest winter on earth' for a concrete impossible-sounding curiosity gap matching the video's own opening hook. Daily evaluator cron (0dedfa05) will auto-judge in ~72h.

policy.notes: User elected fully automatic on 2026-07-04. Agents post replies, launch/revert title tests with no human in loop. Daily evaluator checks for due experiments.
